--- a/rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.tether
+++ b/rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.tether
@@ -29,7 +29,15 @@
     dnsutils        \
     netcat-openbsd  \
     traceroute      \
-    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
+    libcap2-bin     \
+    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
+
+# Grant CAP_NET_RAW to python3 so the unprivileged ifrit user can open
+# raw sockets (required for scapy).  cap_add in compose only places the
+# capability in the bounding set; file capabilities promote it into the
+# effective/permitted sets for non-root execution.
+RUN setcap cap_net_raw+eip /usr/bin/python3

 # Install Claude Code globally (npm, then clean cache)
 RUN npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code --omit=dev \

file.content:
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
#
# Recipe Bottle base compose file — static infrastructure
#
# Invoked via:
#   docker compose --env-file .rbk/rbrr.env --env-file .rbk/{moniker}/rbrn.env \
#     -f .rbk/rbob_compose.yml [-f .rbk/{moniker}/compose.yml] -p {moniker} up -d
#
# Env-file variables serve dual purpose:
#   1. YAML interpolation (${VAR} in this file — image refs, subnet, container names)
#   2. Container environment forwarding (bare names in environment: sections)
#
# Network topology:
#   sentry: default (internet via bridge) + enclave (eth0=default priority:1000, eth1=enclave priority:500)
#   pentacle: enclave only (static IP, routes through sentry)
#   bottle: shares pentacle network namespace (network_mode: service:pentacle)
#
# Health chain: sentry healthy → pentacle starts → pentacle healthy → bottle starts (sessile profile)

services:
  sentry:
    image: ${RBRR_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/rbi_hm/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/image:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}
    container_name: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry
    privileged: true
    networks:
      default:
        priority: 1000
      enclave:
        ipv4_address: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}
        priority: 500
    ports:
      - "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}:${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}"
    environment:
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
      - RBRR_DNS_SERVER
      - RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
      - RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
      - RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
      - RBJE_PROBE
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /tmp/rbjh_healthy"]
      interval: 2s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 15
      start_period: 5s

  pentacle:
    image: ${RBRR_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/rbi_hm/${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}/image:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}
    container_name: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}-pentacle
    privileged: true
    networks:
      enclave:
        ipv4_address: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}
    environment:
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
    command: ["/opt/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh"]
    depends_on:
      sentry:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /tmp/rbjph_healthy"]
      interval: 2s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 15
      start_period: 5s

  bottle:
    image: ${RBRR_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/rbi_hm/${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}/image:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}
    container_name: ${RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX}${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle
    network_mode: "service:pentacle"
    security_opt:
      - label:disable
    cap_drop:
      - NET_RAW
    environment:
      - RBOB_HOST_UID
      - RBOB_HOST_GID
      - RBRV_USER
    depends_on:
      pentacle:
        condition: service_healthy
    profiles:
      - sessile

networks:
  enclave:
    driver: bridge
    ipam:
      config:
        - subnet: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}

content.text:
**Found it. The root cause is missing config, not a binary issue.**

`.rbk/tadmor/compose.yml` has a per-nameplate override:
```yaml
cap_add:
  - NET_RAW
```

This adds NET_RAW back to the bounding set, undoing the base compose's `cap_drop: NET_RAW`. That's why tadmor's rbid (which has file cap `cap_net_raw=ep`) can promote into effective set and exec succeeds.

**Moriah has no `.rbk/moriah/compose.yml`** — `ls .rbk/moriah/` shows only `rbrn.env`. So the base compose's `cap_drop: NET_RAW` applies without override, file caps can't promote, exec → EPERM.

This is a latent moriah-config gap that was hidden behind the charge failure. Now that BBAAX's repair lets charge succeed, the next layer of broken-config surfaces.

content.text:
**Critical finding via git log**: `.rbk/moriah/compose.yml`, `.rbk/srjcl/compose.yml`, `.rbk/pluml/compose.yml` have **never existed** — no commits, no history. Only `.rbk/tadmor/compose.yml` was ever created (in ₢A6AAD / ₢AUAAv).

So this isn't a regression. It's a long-standing structural gap that BBAAX's repair has uncovered. The per-crucible compose.yml files are how each nameplate declares its bottle's runtime config (user, workspace, env_file, volumes, caps). Tadmor has it; moriah/srjcl/pluml never did. They were unreachable runtime-wise because charge died first — now charge succeeds and the next layer of broken config surfaces.

**Three paces' worth of work**, since each crucible needs different content:

| Crucible | Bottle vessel | Tadmor parallel (cap_add NET_RAW, ifrit volumes, Claude env)? | Probable shape |
|---|---|---|---|
| moriah | rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap | YES — same ifrit pattern as tadmor | mostly mirror tadmor's compose.yml |
| srjcl | rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter | partial — Claude env yes, ifrit volumes/cap no | new shape (jupyter workspace) |
| pluml | rbev-bottle-plantuml | partial — no Claude env, no ifrit volumes | new shape (plantuml-specific) |

**This is well outside BBAAX's "tiny inline" triage**. Three new files, each requiring vessel-aware design choices. Recommend:
- Slate as follow-up pace (or paces) under ₣BB
- BBAAX cannot wrap until those land (green tally requires moriah/srjcl/pluml charge → exec → cases pass)
- The new ₢BBABA pace (linux-rerun) would face the same wall on Linux — same compose.yml gap

Or, if you'd prefer: I can mirror tadmor's compose.yml to moriah right now (smallest scope — it's the most-similar shape, ifrit bottle to ifrit bottle), and we get moriah running. Then we'd still need srjcl/pluml work separately. But the per-pace discipline argues against this — three vessels with different shapes = three separate slated paces.

Your call: slate follow-up pace(s), or take the moriah-only inline shortcut?

lastPrompt: yes confirm the arch hypothesis.  I am skeptical.

content.text:
Only bottle. Tadmor's compose fragment has just one `services:` entry:

```yaml
services:
  bottle:
    ...
    cap_add:
      - NET_RAW
```

The base compose's pentacle and sentry are `privileged: true` (full cap set already), so they don't need cap_add. The bottle is the only service the override targets — and the only one whose base config drops caps.

content.text:
**Answer to 1**: Conditional auto-include — `Tools/rbk/rbob_bottle.sh:213`:

```bash
if test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}"; then
  z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}")
fi
```

If `.rbk/{moniker}/compose.yml` exists it gets layered onto the base via a second `-f`. No registration needed — drop the file and it's picked up.

**Answer to 2**: You're right. I conflated two things:
- **NET_RAW** is only needed by ifrit bottles (tadmor, moriah) — the rbid binary's file caps require it.
- **A per-nameplate compose.yml at all** could conceivably be needed by other crucibles for non-cap reasons (workspace volumes, user, env), but that's a different question.

For srjcl (Jupyter) and pluml (PlantUML), the image defaults probably handle user/workdir/entrypoint fine. They run their own commands via `bark`, no rbid involved. They MAY work without any compose.yml. We'd find out by running their fixtures — same way we found moriah's gap by running it.

So the immediate scope shrinks to one file: `.rbk/moriah/compose.yml`, mirroring tadmor's (cap_add NET_RAW + workspace volumes + user + Claude env). After that lands, run the moriah fixture again. If srjcl/pluml fixtures fail later, they get their own diagnosis (likely different mechanism than NET_RAW).

This **does** become "tiny inline" — single file, mostly mirror tadmor with vessel-specific tweaks if any. Want me to create it now?

params.intent: Add missing per-nameplate compose fragment for moriah — second BBAAX surface uncovered by the auto-summon repair (with charge now bootstrapping correctly, the next layer of broken config emerged: 'exec /usr/local/bin/rbid: operation not permitted' on case 3 of moriah's ifrit-attacks section). Diagnosis chain: arch hypothesis ruled out (both ifrit-bottle images are linux/arm64 matching the apple-silicon host); binary inspect identical (794072 bytes, -rwxr-xr-x, file cap cap_net_raw=ep); reproduction in a fresh container with --cap-drop=NET_RAW reproduced the EPERM for both tadmor's AND moriah's binaries; the asymmetry was external — tadmor has .rbk/tadmor/compose.yml with 'cap_add: NET_RAW' that undoes rbob_compose.yml:82-83's 'cap_drop: NET_RAW' on the bottle service. Without that override the file cap can't promote into the effective set (kernel refuses execve with EPERM since file caps cannot exceed the bounding set). Git history confirms .rbk/moriah/compose.yml has never existed (vs .rbk/tadmor/compose.yml landed in A6AAD/AUAAv) — long-standing latent gap that was reachable only after BBAAX's auto-summon repair let charge succeed. Moriah's compose fragment mirrors tadmor's: same user/working_dir/env_file/environment/cap_add/volumes (ifrit bottle, same rbid binary, same Python attack scripts mounted from Tools/rbk/rbtid), with header noting the airgap variant and env_file path swapped to moriah/rbrn.env. srjcl and pluml not addressed — they're non-ifrit bottles (jupyter, plantuml respectively), don't run rbid, and may not need any compose override; if their fixtures fail later they get separate diagnosis. Pace-affiliated to BBAAX since this is the same surface (rbw-tP first-run uncovering broken config that was hidden behind charge-fail) — tiny inline (single file, mirror with one path swap) rather than structural follow-up.
